title: parahebe spathulata
intro: Parahebe spathulata, also known as the native New Zealand bugle, is a flowering plant species native to the islands of New Zealand. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties that have been traditionally used by the indigenous Māori people for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and aiding in digestion. The leaves and stems of the parahebe spathulata plant are typically used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for medicinal use.
